prompt:

title: Hostile save normalization and invalid active-brew recovery
prompt:
Task #20

Work in:

C:\Users\robbi\Documents\Projects\newgame

Do not read archive/.

Run npm.cmd run handoff and confirm it prints Task 20. Work only on Task 20.

Objective: Make current and migratable local-save normalization safe against hostile numeric values and reject a saved active brew whose known recipe is above the normalized saved player level. Preserve legitimate progress and all existing compatibility guarantees.

Inspect first: AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, game-logic.test.cjs, save-compatibility.test.cjs, release-lifecycle.test.cjs, economy-simulation.cjs, package.json, active save fixtures and frozen readers directly required for validation, and only other active files directly required by this task.

Required behavior: Treat every numeric field read from a save as untrusted, including numeric strings, negatives, fractions, non-finite-like strings, extremely large exponential values, Number.MAX_VALUE, and Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. Normalize gameplay counters and values used by arithmetic or UI into finite, nonnegative safe integers with explicit domain-appropriate caps. Preserve existing smaller valid values and established caps. Level must remain between 1 and 10000. XP normalization must terminate in bounded work, never raise level above 10000, and finish in a canonical range below the XP requirement for the resulting level; preserve the existing reasonable multi-level-overflow behavior and remainder. Do not introduce an unbounded per-XP loop. Keep timestamps bounded by their existing rules.

For active brews, accept only a structurally valid known recipe whose unlock level is at or below the normalized saved level. Preserve a valid unlocked brew's recipe, timing, duration, and bounded assist use exactly as current normalization does. If the recipe is known but locked above the saved level, clear only the active brew. Do not raise level, unlock content, refund ingredients, grant a potion, mark discovery or mastery, award XP or currency, or reset unrelated progress. Preserve the existing safe rejection of unknown or malformed brews.

Keep future-version blocking byte-preserving and distinct from recoverable current or older saves. Preserve v1-v7 migration, frozen-reader rollback protection, save schema v8, storage-failure behavior, the four-hour offline cap, and deterministic gameplay/economy behavior. Prefer a small shared normalization boundary and focused tests; do not create a second save parser or broad recovery system. If runtime files change, advance the service-worker cache identity exactly once and make only the factual matching identity updates required by existing automated gates.

Explicitly out of scope: No storage acquisition/read/write/remove redesign; no manual-save or reset changes; no new warning modal or recovery UI; no achievement timing or lifetime-coin accounting change; no Daily Goal, idle-return, Stardust formula, order reward, recipe, upgrade, progression-envelope, gathering, narrative, Journal, After the Stars, tutorial, monetization, account, cloud-save, analytics, native-platform, or broader release work. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, README.md, release/readiness prose, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Do not start another task.

Acceptance checks: Deterministic adversarial tests cover the hostile numeric forms above across representative scalar counters and nested ingredient, potion, mastery, customer, order, daily, weekly, gather, stats, and brew fields. Normalization completes promptly, returns only finite safe bounded values, does not throw, and preserves valid neighboring progress. Reasonable XP overflow still crosses levels and preserves its remainder; extreme XP cannot hang or exceed level 10000. A known above-level active brew is cleared without any compensation or unrelated mutation. A valid unlocked active brew round-trips unchanged, including a completed-but-uncollected brew. Unknown and malformed brews remain safely rejected. Current v8 saves, v1-v7 migration, future-v9 overwrite protection, frozen-reader rollback checks, simulations, and the gather -> brew -> collect -> deliver -> upgrade loop remain unchanged. Save version stays 8 and no new dependency or saved field is added.

Validation: Run npm.cmd run check. In the browser, load a temporary adversarial current-version save without shipping a debug control, confirm the game opens responsively, then exercise gather -> brew -> collect -> deliver -> upgrade. At 390x844 verify safe recovery from an above-level active brew; at 360x740 verify a valid unlocked brew survives reload and can be collected. Confirm no horizontal overflow and no browser console warnings or errors attributable to normalization. Remove temporary QA state before final validation.

Finish with exactly one overall status: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, followed by Summary, Changed files, Validation evidence, Assumptions, and Deferred or out-of-scope findings.

Level never exceeds 10000 and XP normalization is bounded while preserving reasonable overflow semantics
A known above-level active brew is cleared without compensation or unrelated progress loss
A valid unlocked active brew round-trips unchanged and remains collectible when ready

title: Need-aware Request Mix gathering pilot
prompt:
Task #22

Work in:

C:\Users\robbi\Documents\Projects\newgame

Do not read archive/.

Run npm.cmd run handoff and confirm it prints Task 22. Work only on Task 22.

Objective: Make the untargeted charged harvest honest and modestly useful for active requests while preserving the exact targeted-harvest choice, current gather values, and materially stable first-cycle progression.

Inspect first: AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, app.js, index.html, style.css, game-logic.test.cjs, verify.cjs, economy-simulation.cjs, monetization-simulation.cjs, package.json, service-worker.js, service-worker.test.cjs, release-check.cjs, and only other active files directly required by this task.

Required behavior: Rename the untargeted charged option and button copy from Smart Mix to Request Mix. Describe it truthfully as random items weighted toward active requests; do not promise needed items or recipe completion. Keep selecting an ingredient as the exact existing targeted-harvest guarantee. For Request Mix only, derive outstanding bottle demand from current valid active orders by recipe, subtract bottled potions already available for those orders, aggregate the remaining recipes' ingredient requirements, and subtract current Pantry stock. On each ingredient roll, recompute a deterministic weighted pool containing every unlocked ingredient with base weight 1 plus one extra entry per positive missing unit capped at 3 extras per ingredient. Thus a needed ingredient has weight 2 through 4, every unlocked ingredient remains possible, and no ingredient is guaranteed. If no valid active request has a positive ingredient deficit, use the current uniform unlocked-ingredient mix. Keep injected RNG behavior deterministic. Passive and offline gathering must remain on their current path and must not receive Request Mix weighting. Preserve three charges, 30-second recharge, base yield 3, upgrade effects, storage limits, targeting, discard behavior, tutorial progression, audio, achievements, and save shape/version.

Update the null-target selection toast to say that Request Mix favors missing ingredients but stays random. Add focused tests for one request deficit, multiple and duplicate requested recipes, bottled-potion allocation, existing Pantry stock, locked or malformed orders, no-deficit uniform fallback, deterministic weighted boundaries, exact targeted gathering, storage capacity, and unchanged passive/offline behavior. Compare the implementation against the Task 21 first-cycle baselines: seed 7 = 2,660 seconds and 31 orders; seed 42 = 2,640 seconds and 31 orders; seed 2026 = 2,540 seconds and 32 orders. Each seed may vary by at most 5% in completion time and one order. Record the before/after comparison. Do not retune rewards, timers, recipes, upgrades, gathering values, or progression to force a result. Keep recipe selection, newest-recipe behavior, quantity, reward, XP, note generation, order IDs, board size, reserved-slot arbitration, Workshop shortcut, refresh cost, fulfillment, friendship favors, Mira's narrative pilot, Journal stories, special-request invitations, keepsakes, and After the Stars unchanged. title: Meaningful-choice measurement harness
prompt:
Task #25

Work in:

C:\Users\robbi\Documents\Projects\newgame

Do not read archive/.

Run npm.cmd run handoff and confirm it prints Task 25. Work only on Task 25.

Objective: Create a reproducible, development-only measurement harness that shows whether the four existing same-level potion pairs and five existing upgrades produce meaningfully different outcomes. This task gathers evidence for one later bounded design decision; it must not tune or implement gameplay.

Inspect first: AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, game-logic.test.cjs, economy-simulation.cjs, monetization-simulation.cjs, package.json, release-check.cjs, release-budgets.json, and only other active files directly required for a development-only simulation.

Required artifact: Add one focused choice-simulation.cjs harness using the shipped game-logic module, the existing LCG convention, fixed UTC time, and seeds 7, 42, 2026, 99, and 1234. Add syntax and execution of this harness to npm.cmd run check. Running it twice must produce byte-for-byte identical stdout. It must use current public game-logic behavior rather than copying or reimplementing reward, gathering, brewing, upgrade, storage, or order formulas. Temporary state construction may set up a scenario, but all measured actions must use the shipped functions.

Recipe-pair measurements: Cover Bottled Sunrise versus Lantern Sip at level 4, Kindheart Cordial versus Quietbell Tea at level 5, Dreamer's Draught versus Wayfinder Cordial at level 6, and Starlight Philter versus Aurora Nectar at level 7. For each pair, print the current timer, base sell value, total ingredient units, and ingredient identities. Run matched seeded request scenarios from equal zero-stock, zero-potion states with one valid one-bottle ordinary order for the measured recipe, passive gathering enabled as it is after the first delivery, no upgrades, Request Mix charged gathering, immediate brewing when affordable, collection when ready, and delivery when possible. Measure time to first affordable brew, first collection, first delivery, charged items granted, passive items granted, final Pantry stock, and whether the request completes within a ten-minute limit. Keep the order/customer valid and do not award setup progress or rewards. Use the same seeds and scenario rules for both recipes in a pair.

Upgrade measurements: Cover Moonlit Garden, Bottomless Basket, Copper Cauldron, Pantry Shelves, and Golden Ledger. Compare an otherwise identical level-4 state with that upgrade at level 0 versus level 1. Use a documented representative one-bottle ordinary-order loop, passive gathering enabled, Request Mix, normal charged-harvest timing, normal brewing, collection, delivery, and current upgrade formulas. Report matched-seed deltas over ten active minutes for ingredients granted, completed brews, completed orders, spendable coins, lifetime coins, maximum Pantry occupancy, and time spent blocked by storage or brewing where measurable. Also run 60-minute and 120-minute offline-return comparisons using the shipped offline function and report granted ingredients, final stock, and capacity; do not reinterpret the four-hour safety cap or change idle behavior. If an upgrade has zero effect in a scenario, report zero rather than inventing a favorable scenario.

Output and interpretation: Print compact JSON or stable tables with one row per seed plus an average for every recipe and upgrade scenario. Include a final factual summary listing exact ties, measurable differences, and any choice that has zero measured marginal effect in these scenarios. Do not label a current option good, bad, balanced, or dominated unless the numeric definition used is printed. Findings are evidence and do not authorize a gameplay change. A surprising or zero result is not a task failure.

Add structural assertions that all required pairs, upgrades, seeds, active windows, and offline windows are present; all results are finite and nonnegative; paired scenarios begin from equivalent state except for the named recipe/order or upgrade; the source state is not reused across comparison arms; and a second in-process run serializes identically. Do not assert that one option must win or weaken existing economy, monetization, compatibility, release, or budget checks. Preserve the exact owner-approved Task 24 first-cycle outputs.

Explicitly out of scope: No change to game-logic.js, app.js, index.html, style.css, service-worker.js, save schema, recipes, ingredients, timers, sell values, costs, rewards, XP, trust, orders, upgrades, gathering, offline formulas, Stardust, Daily Goals, rolling requests, achievements, mastery, cosmetics, narrative, UI, assets, dependencies, release evidence, release budgets, cache identity, or production behavior. Do not create a balance recommendation document. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, README.md, release/readiness prose, or archive/. Do not stage, commit, or push. Do not start another task.

Acceptance checks: The development-only harness covers all four named recipe pairs, all five upgrades, all five seeds, ten-minute active comparisons, and 60/120-minute offline comparisons. It calls shipped game-logic functions for measured behavior, starts paired arms equivalently, emits finite stable metrics and a neutral factual summary, and produces identical serialized output on a repeated run. Existing Task 24 exact simulation locks and all existing checks remain unchanged. No runtime file, cache identity, release budget, save behavior, or gameplay value changes.

Validation: Run npm.cmd run check. Run node choice-simulation.cjs twice and compare the complete outputs for byte equality. Browser QA is not required because this task may change only a development-only .cjs harness and package.json test wiring. If any runtime or browser-facing file changes, report FAIL and restore that change before finishing.

Finish with exactly one overall status: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, followed by Summary, Changed files, Validation evidence, a concise Findings table covering all four recipe pairs and five upgrades, Assumptions, and Deferred or out-of-scope findings.
acceptance: A reproducible development-only harness covers all four same-level recipe pairs and all five upgrades
Measurements use seeds 7, 42, 2026, 99, and 1234 with fixed time and shipped game-logic functions
Recipe scenarios report availability, collection, delivery, gathering, and Pantry outcomes over ten minutes
Upgrade scenarios report matched active deltas and 60/120-minute offline outcomes
Paired arms begin equivalently, use isolated state, and return finite nonnegative results
Repeated execution produces byte-for-byte identical output and a neutral factual summary
Exact Task 24 progression locks and all existing automated checks remain unchanged
No runtime, gameplay value, save, cache, budget, UI, asset, or release-evidence file changes
The coder reports changed files, validation, a findings table, assumptions, deferred findings, and one PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED status
